tasks.description: NetworkPolicy type, Zod schema, mode preset resolver, egress checker, and PolicyViolationError
steps: Read PRD §10 carefully — mode presets table, enforcement architecture, egress rules, host matching
Create src/core/network-policy.ts
Define NetworkPolicySchema (Zod) matching PRD §10 / Appendix A config structure
Define EgressTarget interface: { host, port?, protocol, description }
Define PolicyCheckResult: { allowed: true } | { allowed: false, reason: string }
Define ResolvedEgressRules: { allowDns, allowMqttHub, allowedHosts, unrestricted }
Define ResolvedIngressRules: { allowLocalWebui, allowLocalApi, allowedCidrs }
Implement MODE_PRESETS constant with defaults for all 3 modes per PRD §10 table
Implement resolveNetworkPolicy(raw): parse schema + merge mode defaults with user overrides
Implement NetworkPolicy class: constructor takes resolved rules, freezes self
Implement checkEgress(target): unrestricted check → DNS check → allowMqttHub check → allowedHosts check → mode-based denial
Implement summary(): human-readable one-liner for startup log (e.g. '🏠 LOCAL NETWORK — egress: 2 allowed hosts, no DNS, no Hub')
Implement PolicyViolationError class extending Error with target + reason + mode in message
Implement parseHostPort(entry: string): { host, port? } for allowed_hosts matching
Write tests in test/unit/core/network-policy.test.ts
Test all 3 mode presets resolve to correct defaults
Test user overrides merge on top of mode defaults (e.g. local_network + allow_dns=true)
Test checkEgress: hostname denied when allowDns=false
Test checkEgress: hub broker denied when allowMqttHub=false
Test checkEgress: IP:port in allowedHosts → allowed
Test checkEgress: IP:port NOT in allowedHosts → denied with reason
Test checkEgress: host-only match (any port)
Test checkEgress: connected mode → always allowed (unrestricted)
Test checkEgress: standalone mode → all denied (empty allowedHosts)
Test checkEgress: local_network with explicit allowedHosts → allowed for matching, denied for non-matching
Test summary() returns different strings for each mode
Test ingress rules are parsed and accessible (not enforced)
Test PolicyViolationError message format includes mode, target, reason
Run bun test — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-8: NetworkPolicy type, schema, preset resolver, and egress checker

tasks.description: Config parser integration — parse [network_policy] section and include NetworkPolicy in AgentConfig
steps: Read src/core/config.ts to understand current parseConfig() flow
Import NetworkPolicy, resolveNetworkPolicy, NetworkPolicySchema from network-policy.ts
Add networkPolicy: NetworkPolicy to AgentConfig interface
In parseConfig(): extract raw.network_policy section from parsed TOML
Parse with NetworkPolicySchema.safeParse() — report validation errors same format as agent section
Call resolveNetworkPolicy() to produce the NetworkPolicy instance
Include in returned AgentConfig
If [network_policy] is absent: default to connected mode (resolveNetworkPolicy with empty input)
Add hub/policy conflict warning: if agent.hub.enabled && !networkPolicy.egress.allowMqttHub, log warning during parsing
Update config-validate.ts: print resolved network policy in validation output
Write tests in test/unit/core/config.test.ts (add to existing test file)
Test: config with [network_policy] section parses correctly
Test: config without [network_policy] defaults to connected mode
Test: config with mode + egress overrides resolves correctly
Test: invalid mode string produces Zod validation error
Test: hub enabled + policy blocks hub → produces warning (not hard error)
Update test/unit/cli/config-validate.test.ts: policy shown in validate output
Run bun test — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-8: parse [network_policy] config section into AgentConfig

tasks.description: Output plugin enforcement — MQTT output validates targets against NetworkPolicy at connect()
steps: Read src/plugins/outputs/mqtt.ts — understand the two connect() paths (sparkplug vs plain)
Read src/pipeline/plugin-factory.ts — understand how outputs are constructed
Add networkPolicy?: NetworkPolicy as optional constructor parameter to MqttOutput
In MqttOutput.connect() for PLAIN mode: before connecting, parse each server URL into EgressTarget, call policy.checkEgress(), throw PolicyViolationError if denied

In MqttOutput.connect() for SPARKPLUG mode: validate the hub broker URL (from hub link config) against policy. The hub broker URL needs to flow through — either via the hub link instance or passed as a config field. Choose the simplest approach: pass the broker URL as an optional constructor param alongside hubLink.

For sparkplug mode validation: if hubLink is provided, the hub broker URL was already validated (by config parsing). But for defense-in-depth, also validate at connect time. Get the broker URL from a new optional field on MqttOutput config or pass through the constructor.

Actually — simplest approach: the MQTT output in sparkplug mode doesn't connect its own client. The Hub link does. So sparkplug mode enforcement should happen in plugin-factory.ts BEFORE creating the hub link, or in the hub link constructor. See 8.2b below.
For PLAIN mode: parse URL(server) → extract hostname and port → build EgressTarget → check
Handle URL parsing: mqtt://, mqtts://, tcp:// schemes — URL constructor may need scheme mapping (mqtt: → tcp: for parsing)
In plugin-factory.ts buildPipeline(): pass config.networkPolicy to MqttOutput constructor

In plugin-factory.ts buildPipeline(): BEFORE creating HubLink, validate hub broker URL against policy. If denied, throw PolicyViolationError immediately. Don't create a HubLink that will fail to connect.
In runtime.ts start(): add a log line at the very beginning: logger.info('network policy', { mode: policy.mode, summary: policy.summary() })
Pass networkPolicy through PipelineOptions so runtime.ts can access it for logging
Add networkPolicy?: NetworkPolicy to PipelineOptions interface
Write tests in test/unit/plugins/outputs/mqtt.test.ts (add to existing)
Test: MQTT output plain mode with standalone policy → connect() throws PolicyViolationError
Test: MQTT output plain mode with local_network + server not in allowedHosts → throws
Test: MQTT output plain mode with local_network + server in allowedHosts → does not throw (proceeds to connect)
Test: MQTT output plain mode with connected policy → does not throw
Test: MQTT output without networkPolicy (undefined) → no enforcement (backward compat)
Test: PolicyViolationError message includes target host, port, mode, and reason
Write tests in test/unit/pipeline/plugin-factory.test.ts (add to existing)
Test: buildPipeline with hub enabled + standalone policy → throws PolicyViolationError
Test: buildPipeline with hub enabled + connected policy → creates hub link normally
Test: buildPipeline passes networkPolicy to MQTT output
Run bun test — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-8: enforce network policy on MQTT output and hub link at startup

tasks.description: Config validate command enhancement — report resolved policy, flag conflicts as warnings
steps: Read src/cli/commands/config-validate.ts to understand current output format
Enhance validation output to include a 'Network Policy' section showing: mode, resolved egress rules, resolved ingress rules
Add conflict detection: scan configured outputs against the resolved policy
For each output that would be blocked: emit a WARNING line (not an error — config is syntactically valid)
Specific warnings: 'Hub is enabled but network_policy blocks Hub connectivity', 'MQTT output server X:Y not in network_policy.egress.allowed_hosts'
Warning format: 'WARNING: <description>. The pipeline will fail to start with this configuration.'
To detect output conflicts without instantiating plugins: check config.outputs for mqtt entries, parse their server URLs or check sparkplug mode against policy
Update test/unit/cli/config-validate.test.ts
Test: validate output includes network policy section
Test: hub + standalone → warning about hub blocked
Test: mqtt output with remote server + local_network (no allowedHosts) → warning
Test: local_store only + standalone → clean validation, no warnings
Test: connected mode + hub + mqtt → clean validation
Run bun test — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-8: config validate reports network policy and flags conflicts

tasks.description: Integration tests — full startup enforcement, config init templates, end-to-end policy wiring
steps: Create test/integration/network-policy-enforcement.test.ts
Test: parse standalone TOML config → build pipeline with mock MQTT output → startup fails with PolicyViolationError
Test: parse local_network TOML config with local_store only → startup succeeds
Test: parse connected TOML config with hub + MQTT sparkplug output → startup succeeds (with mock MQTT client)
Test: parse local_network TOML with MQTT output targeting allowed host → startup succeeds
Test: parse local_network TOML with MQTT output targeting disallowed host → startup fails with PolicyViolationError
Test: hub enabled + local_network mode → fails during pipeline build (before runtime start)
Test: network policy summary logged at startup (capture logger output)
Update test/unit/cli/config-init.test.ts
Test: all 3 mode templates generate valid configs that parse without error
Test: standalone template does not include active (uncommented) MQTT outputs
Test: connected template config passes validation without policy warnings
Run bun test — ALL tests pass (full suite, every test file)
Update plans/phase-8-progress.md with final test count and commit hash
Commit: phase-8: integration tests for network policy enforcement
